title: Three-tier failure-domain escalation
scenario: A production web platform uses one external load balancer, four application servers in one region, and a primary PostgreSQL database with one hot standby in a second availability zone. A recent outage exposed cascading failure between app retries and database failover. Leadership requests a failure-domain analysis and safer recovery plan.
constraints: Identify failure domains before proposing remediations.
Call out single points of failure and failover boundaries.
Avoid claiming resilience that is not evidenced by topology details.

required_facts.description: Load balancer, app tier, and database tier are distinct domains.
Database primary/standby behavior must be analyzed for failover lag and split-brain risk.
Recovery guidance should include verification and rollback conditions.

expected_escalation_conditions: Cross-tier cascading failures require deeper review.
Unclear failover orchestration requires specialist escalation.

shared_rubric_rules.description: Load balancer, app tier, and database tier are distinct domains.
Database primary/standby behavior must be analyzed for failover lag and split-brain risk.
Recovery guidance should include verification and rollback conditions.
Rejects recommendations that violate scenario safety constraints.

phrases: claim full resilience without topology evidence
